summary: Completed comprehensive testing of new PaperSumo features: 1) Keywords as tournament title - search_query is now used as category_name instead of 'Custom Selection', 2) Citation counts from Semantic Scholar API displayed in search results, tournament page, and results page, 3) Confidence level affects estimated comparison count with formula: 1 + (confidence_level - 0.80) * 3. All features working correctly. Fixed minor bug: results API endpoint was missing papers.citation_count field.

minor.fix_applied: Added 'papers.citation_count': 1 to results endpoint projection (server.py line 1375)

passed_tests: Search returns citation_count field for papers
Citation counts fetched from Semantic Scholar API
search_query becomes category_name for custom tournaments
Tournament list shows keywords as title
No 'Search:' prefix in history page titles
Higher confidence (99%) results in more estimated comparisons than lower (80%)
Results API returns papers with citation_count
Search results display citation badges (amber with quote icon)
Tournament page paper list shows citation badges
Results page top 3 podium shows citation badges
Results page complete rankings show citation badges
Tournament title uses keywords instead of 'Custom Selection'
Results page shows keywords as category name
seed_data_creation: Created and deleted multiple test tournaments during testing. One tournament 'keywords: quantum computing' left for visual verification.
context_for_next_testing_agent: New features fully implemented and tested: 1) Keywords as title - when creating tournament from search, search_query is used as category_name (visible in History, Tournament, and Results pages). 2) Citation counts - fetched from Semantic Scholar batch API (/graph/v1/paper/batch) and displayed as amber badges with quote icon. Citation counts may be null for papers not in Semantic Scholar. 3) Confidence affects estimates - formula is 1 + (confidence_level - 0.80) * 3, so 80% = 1.0x, 95% = 1.45x, 99% = 1.57x multiplier on estimated comparisons. Key test file: /app/tests/test_citation_keywords_confidence.py
